summary: All three requirements (REQ-36, REQ-37, REQ-38) appear to be implemented in the diff. SubscriptionPage.tsx was rebuilt with the invite code UI, backend/main.mo has a recordPayment that sets status to #active without amount checks, and useQueries.ts has the useActivateByInviteCode hook. Minor concerns: stable storage for subscriptions is not explicitly confirmed in the truncated diff, and legacy ICP Ledger types remain in the backend.

missing_requirements.requirement: Subscription records survive canister upgrades (stored in stable memory).
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows 'let subscriptions = Map.empty<Principal, SubscriptionRecord>()' but does not show any stable var declaration or preupgrade/postupgrade hooks to persist the subscriptions map across canister upgrades. No evidence of stable storage is present in the visible diff.

hallucinated_features.description: Retained ICP Ledger type definitions (AccountIdentifier, Tokens, Memo, Transfer, Operation) and potentially other legacy ICP Ledger inter-canister call code in backend/main.mo.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request non-goals explicitly state removing ICP Ledger transfer and block-index verification logic, yet the truncated diff shows these types are still present in the actor file.

hallucinated_features.description: useRecordPayment hook retained with a bigint amount parameter that is silently ignored when calling actor.recordPayment() with no arguments.
hallucinated_features.reason: This hook was not requested to be retained or modified; the new useActivateByInviteCode hook was the requested replacement, making useRecordPayment with its unused amount parameter an unrequested addition.
